rural_site_scaling: Biodiversity: boundary reinforcement (<100 m²); 20% set aside (100-300 m²); 50% set aside (>300 m²)
separation_distance_m: Minimum 22 m between opposing first-floor windows at rear; 22 m between dwellings and schools/childcare
building_height: Addressed via DM process (§2.4) applying national Building Heights Guidelines / SPPRs
trigger_AA: AA screening for no likely significant effects on Natura 2000 sites; full AA where effects not excluded (§1.5.1)
trigger_EIA: EIA above thresholds; sub-threshold EIA where warranted; EIAR required where EIA applies (§1.5.2)
trigger_FRA: Flood-risk management per 2009 Guidelines; Justification Test for development in flood zones
trigger_AHIA: Protected Structures (§7.2) / ACAs (§7.3); AHIA expectation for works affecting protected structures/curtilage
trigger_TTA: TTA above tabulated thresholds: Tables 6-1 (mandatory), 6-2 (advisory national roads), 6-3 (sub-threshold)
notes: Rural site sizes are tabulated (Table 3-3) — one of few councils with explicit numeric rural minimums. Dual-aspect min 33% (objective generally 50%). Min 20% of dwellings to be a defined unit type. Apartments per New Apartments 2020 Design Standards.
